Package net.apartium.cocoabeans.schematic

Interface PasteOperation

@NullMarked @AvailableSince("0.0.46") public interface PasteOperation
Represents a schematic paste operation
See Also:
  • Schematic
  • PasteResult

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    PasteResult
    advanceAllAxis(int numOfBlocks)
    Paste specified-number of blocks on all axis (X, Y, Z), advancing by the order of the axis
    PasteResult
    advanceOnDualAxis(int numOfBlocks)
    Paste specified-number of blocks on dual axis For example, if the axis order is AxisOrder.XYZ, the paste will be on the Y and Z axis simultaniously
    PasteResult
    advanceOnSingleAxis(int numOfBlocks)
    Paste specified number of blocks on single axis For example, if the axis order is AxisOrder.XYZ, the paste will be on the Z axis
    @Nullable net.apartium.cocoabeans.space.Position
    getNextPosition()
    Returns the next placement position for this operation.
    boolean
    hasNext()
    Check if there are any more blocks to paste
    PasteResult
    performAll()
    Paste all remaining blocks in their natural order
    PasteResult
    performAllOnDualAxis()
    Paste all remaining blocks on dual axis For example, if the axis order is AxisOrder.XYZ, the paste will be on the Y and Z axis simultaniously
    PasteResult
    performAllOnSingleAxis()
    Paste all remaining blocks on single axis.

  • Method Details

    • hasNext

      boolean hasNext()
      Check if there are any more blocks to paste
      Returns:
      whether there are blocks remaining in this operation

    • getNextPosition

      @Nullable @Nullable net.apartium.cocoabeans.space.Position getNextPosition()
      Returns the next placement position for this operation. This method does not alter the state of the operation
      Returns:
      next paste position

    • performAll

      PasteResult performAll()
      Paste all remaining blocks in their natural order
      Returns:
      paste result

    • performAllOnSingleAxis

      PasteResult performAllOnSingleAxis()
      Paste all remaining blocks on single axis. For example, if the axis order is AxisOrder.XYZ, the paste will be on the Z axis
      Returns:
      paste result
      See Also:
      • AxisOrder

    • performAllOnDualAxis

      PasteResult performAllOnDualAxis()
      Paste all remaining blocks on dual axis For example, if the axis order is AxisOrder.XYZ, the paste will be on the Y and Z axis simultaniously
      Returns:
      paste result

    • advanceAllAxis

      PasteResult advanceAllAxis(int numOfBlocks)
      Paste specified-number of blocks on all axis (X, Y, Z), advancing by the order of the axis
      Parameters:
      numOfBlocks - number of blocks to paste
      Returns:
      paste result

    • advanceOnDualAxis

      PasteResult advanceOnDualAxis(int numOfBlocks)
      Paste specified-number of blocks on dual axis For example, if the axis order is AxisOrder.XYZ, the paste will be on the Y and Z axis simultaniously
      Parameters:
      numOfBlocks - number of blocks to paste
      Returns:
      paste result
      See Also:
      • AxisOrder

    • advanceOnSingleAxis

      PasteResult advanceOnSingleAxis(int numOfBlocks)
      Paste specified number of blocks on single axis For example, if the axis order is AxisOrder.XYZ, the paste will be on the Z axis
      Parameters:
      numOfBlocks - number of blocks to paste
      Returns:
      paste result
      See Also:
      • AxisOrder
